candidates.name: prior-fold-performance-regime-consistency

economic_rationale: A credible adaptive research process should not require exclusively favorable or unfavorable immediately preceding OOS performance. This prior-only regime diagnostic tests whether current-fold returns persist after both observed operating states.
failure_reasons: BTC-USDT previous_positive current-fold mean 95% lower bound is non-positive: -0.133355511986
BTC-USDT previous_nonpositive current-fold mean 95% lower bound is non-positive: -0.074669357906
ETH-USDT previous_positive current-fold mean 95% lower bound is non-positive: -0.185310268995
ETH-USDT previous_nonpositive current-fold mean 95% lower bound is non-positive: -0.057386368138
hypothesis: For both BTC-USDT and ETH-USDT, net rolling OOS returns have a positive annualized arithmetic mean after both a positive and a non-positive immediately preceding OOS fold, with all four 95% moving-block-bootstrap lower bounds above zero.
limitations: BTC-USDT and ETH-USDT are development markets, not untouched holdouts.
Only 25 current folds per market have an immediately preceding OOS fold.
Moving-block concatenation creates artificial fold joins, although prior-fold performance labels are recomputed after each join.
The experiment diagnoses dependence on recent OOS performance; it does not introduce a trading rule or retune any strategy parameter.
The analysis does not model spread, impact, capacity, latency, or partial fills.

method.first_fold_handling: excluded because no prior OOS fold exists
method.fold_block_rationale: three adjacent 90-day folds preserve roughly nine months of local OOS dependence while retaining 24 admissible non-circular block starts
method.metric: conditional daily net-return arithmetic mean multiplied by 365
method.resampling: paired non-circular moving blocks over complete observed 90-day fold records; previous-fold performance labels are recomputed after block concatenation
